nativeRange.habitat: Shallow marine coastal waters; found on rocky substrates and reef areas in the photic zone

avoidWith: aggressive herbivores that may consume it

imageCandidates.notes: Product photo from Mosaic Macros listing for Red Bristle Comb Macroalgae (Alsidium triquetrum).
Product photo from Mosaic Macros listing for Red Razor Macroalgae (sw-algae-082 merge candidate; same species Bryothamnion triquetrum = Alsidium triquetrum, different SKU).

summary: A decorative red macroalgae from the Caribbean with a distinctive branching form and serrated, bristle-like edges. Generally kept as a display specimen in established reef aquariums.
careNotes: Alsidium triquetrum (synonym: Bryothamnion triquetrum) is a red macroalgae in family Rhodomelaceae, order Ceramiales. Also sold by Mosaic Macros under the trade name 'Red Razor' (SKU sw-algae-082); both the Red Bristle Comb and Red Razor SKUs describe a branching red alga with serrated edges and cite the same scientific name — they represent the same species. As of 2026 it is sold through Mosaic Macros; no AlgaeBarn or Top Shelf Aquatics listings were found, making Mosaic the sole documented hobby source. Specific aquarium care data is limited in the hobby literature — the following is extrapolated from family-level characteristics of Rhodomelaceae and general tropical reef macroalgae husbandry. Medium lighting is generally advised (comparable to other ornamental red macros in the family); full-spectrum LED is preferred over actinic-only spectrums, which Mosaic explicitly notes cannot support more demanding species. Growth rate tends to be medium for Rhodomelaceae filamentous forms. It has been documented in Caribbean and tropical Atlantic waters (Barbados, Martinique, Curaçao, Colombia, Brazil, Cape Verde), so standard reef parameters (salinity SG 1.023–1.026, temperature 22–28°C, pH 8.1–8.4) are appropriate. As a red macroalgae it should NOT be placed directly in the sand bed — Mosaic's care guide notes that red and brown algae will melt at the base if buried in sand. Recommended attachment methods are zip-tie/fishing line, wedging between rocks, or superglue (though Mosaic notes superglue is the least preferred long-term method as it can cause basal melting). Nutrient uptake is low relative to refugium-grade species such as Chaetomorpha — it is best suited as an ornamental display piece rather than a nutrient-export workhorse. Weekly dosing of iron, iodine, and broad-spectrum trace elements is generally advised for all macroalgae per Mosaic. Flow should be gentle and comparable to a soft-coral tank; excessive flow can damage the delicate thallus. Propagation is typically achieved by fragmentation. The species is generally considered reef-safe and compatible with typical reef invertebrates. Scientific literature (≥140 papers indexed on Google Scholar) documents it as a component of Caribbean shallow reef communities and as a subject of agar-production studies.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby trade source identified. Confirms species identity as Alsidium triquetrum, describes branching red macroalgae with serrated edges, sold as 1–2" cutting or 5"+ cutting.

additional.name: World Register of Marine Species (WoRMS)
additional.notes: Confirmed accepted taxonomy: Family Rhodomelaceae, Order Ceramiales, Class Florideophyceae, Phylum Rhodophyta. Synonyms include Bryothamnion triquetrum (M.Howe, 1915) and Fucus triqueter S.G.Gmelin, 1768 (basionym).

additional.name: NCBI Taxonomy Browser
additional.notes: Confirmed Family Rhodomelaceae, Order Ceramiales. Homotypic synonym Bryothamnion triquetrum (S.G.Gmelin) M.Howe 1915 noted.

additional.name: Google Scholar — distribution literature
additional.notes: Multiple peer-reviewed papers confirm Caribbean and tropical Atlantic distribution (Barbados, Martinique, Curaçao, Colombia, Brazil, Cape Verde). Species studied for temperature responses and agar production.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Red Razor product page
additional.notes: Second Mosaic SKU (sw-algae-082) for the same species. Identical morphology described: branching with serrated edges. Merged into this entry; trade name 'Red Razor' added to alsoKnownAs.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Macroalgae Care Guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source. Confirms water parameters: temp 22–26°C (72–78°F), pH 8.1–8.4, NO3 5–20 ppm, PO4 0.02–0.1 ppm, Ca 400–450 ppm, Mg 1250–1350 ppm. Explicitly warns red algae will melt at base if buried in sand; recommends zip-tie, fishing line, or wedging for attachment. Recommends weekly iron + iodine + trace element dosing.
